title: Ravi's Silly Sock Day
synopsis: When little Ravi can't find matching socks, he discovers that wearing silly mismatched socks makes everyone happy and leads to a fun family dance party.

pages.text: Ravi woke up feeling happy! Today was going to be a great day. He brushed his teeth, combed his black hair, and got dressed. But when he opened his sock drawer, he felt confused. Where were his matching socks?

illustration.description: A cheerful South Asian boy with short black hair and brown skin stands in his bright bedroom wearing a yellow t-shirt and blue pants, looking puzzled at an open dresser drawer. His room has colorful toys scattered around and sunshine streaming through the window. The illustration uses bold primary colors with a textured, construction paper-like background.
illustration.alt: Ravi looking confused at his sock drawer in his colorful bedroom

pages.text: Ravi looked everywhere! Under his bed, behind his toy box, in the laundry basket. He found one red sock with stripes, one blue sock with polka dots, and one green sock with stars. But no matching pairs! Ravi felt sad and frustrated.

illustration.description: Ravi crouched down searching under his bed with a worried expression, holding three different colorful socks in his hands. Toys are scattered around and he looks determined but upset. His bedroom is warm and cozy with rounded furniture in bright colors against a textured yellow background.
illustration.alt: Ravi searching for socks under his bed, holding mismatched colorful socks

pages.text: Mama came into his room and saw Ravi looking upset. 'What's wrong, beta?' she asked gently. Ravi showed her the mismatched socks. 'I can't find any that match!' he said sadly. Mama smiled and had a wonderful idea.

illustration.description: A kind South Asian woman with long dark hair in a purple sari kneels beside Ravi, who looks sad while holding up the three different socks. She has a warm, understanding expression and gentle smile. The scene is set against a soft blue textured background with morning light filtering in.
illustration.alt: Mama comforting sad Ravi while he shows her the mismatched socks

pages.text: 'What if we wear silly socks today?' Mama suggested. 'You could wear the red striped sock and the blue polka dot sock!' Ravi felt curious about this idea. Could wearing different socks actually be fun?

illustration.description: Mama holds up the red striped sock and blue polka dot sock with an excited expression while Ravi looks intrigued, his sad expression changing to curiosity. The colorful socks are prominently displayed between them against a warm orange textured background.
illustration.alt: Mama showing Ravi how to wear mismatched socks as a fun choice

pages.text: Ravi put on the silly mismatched socks and looked in the mirror. They looked so funny and colorful! He started to giggle. Then he felt brave and decided to wear them to breakfast. Papa and big sister Priya thought his socks were amazing!

illustration.description: Ravi stands proudly in front of a mirror wearing his mismatched red striped and blue polka dot socks, giggling with joy. His reflection shows his happy face and the colorful socks clearly. The mirror has a friendly rounded frame against a cheerful green textured background.
illustration.alt: Happy Ravi looking at himself in the mirror wearing silly mismatched socks

pages.text: 'Silly Sock Dance!' shouted Papa, and the whole family started dancing in the kitchen. Mama found mismatched socks too! Even baby cousin Arjun clapped his hands. Ravi felt so happy and proud. Sometimes being different is the most fun of all!

illustration.description: The whole family dances joyfully in a bright kitchen - Ravi in his silly socks, Papa in a white shirt, Mama in her sari also wearing mismatched socks, teenage Priya with braided hair, and baby Arjun clapping. Everyone has big smiles and expressive, happy faces against a warm yellow textured background.
illustration.alt: The entire family dancing happily in the kitchen, all wearing silly mismatched socks

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Ravi feel when he couldn't find matching socks?
questions.explanation: Ravi felt sad and frustrated because he couldn't find any socks that matched.

questions.question: Who gave Ravi the idea to wear silly socks?
questions.explanation: Mama suggested that Ravi could wear silly mismatched socks and make it fun.

questions.question: What did the family do at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Papa started a Silly Sock Dance and the whole family joined in the fun.

questions.question: How did Ravi feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Ravi felt happy and proud when he realized that being different can be the most fun of all.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instruction: Choose the right word to complete each sentence from Ravi's story!
pages.text: Ravi woke up feeling ___(1)___! But when he opened his sock drawer, he felt ___(2)___. Mama suggested wearing ___(3)___ socks instead!

pages.title: More Missing Words
pages.instruction: Pick the best word to finish these sentences!
pages.text: Ravi felt ___(1)___ about trying something new. The whole ___(2)___ joined the Silly Sock Dance. Ravi learned that being different can be ___(3)___!
